Administration

322·6 .... 1 
Statements of Economic Interest 
322-6444 
(916) 322·5662 
Senator Becky Morgan 
State Capitol, Room 4090 
~acramento, CA 
95814 
uear ~enacor Morgan: 
322.5660 
322·5901 
March 1, 1985 
~e: 
Uur TeLephone Conversation 
~1Le NO. 
A-H~-048 
'l'n1s Letter 1S sent to confirm our telephone conversation 
of February 21, 1985, during which I answered several questions 
on your financial disclosure obligations as an elected state 
official under the Political Reform Act.ll 
I advised you that you were not required to report an 
interest in a vacation cabin so long as it is exclusively used 
by you and your family and no income is derived from it. 
Section 87206 (as amended by Stats. 1984, Ch. 931). 
This 
exception would also apply to any other personal residence 
including a home in Sacramento. 
You also asked me how to report the sale of stock during 
the reporting period. 
On Schedule A (Investments), you should 
list the stock and indicate the date of disposition. 
On 
Schedule D (Income), you should list the sale of stock and check 
the box which corresponds to the sale amount; if it is stock 
traded on a public exchange, you do not need to identify the 
actual purchaser unless it is otherwise known to you.~1 
II The Act is contained in Government Code Sections 
81000-91015. 
All statutory references are to the Government 
Code. 
~I Please note that dividends paid on stock which is 
registered with the Securities and Exchange Commission are not 
reportable. 
Section 82030 (b) (5). 


Senator Becky Morgan 
March 1, 1985 
Page 2 
Lastly, I advised you that investments in, and income from, 
any type of government issued bonds are not reportable. 
Sections 82030 (b) (4) and 82034. 
I also discussed with you some of the other recent 
legislative changes in the area of financial disclosure. 
I told 
you that the definition of income had been amended to exclude 
second mortgages on an official's principal place of residence 
which are taken out to refinance or make improvements to the 
residence. 
See Stats. 1984, Ch. 931 (copy enclosed). 
However, 
I did not tell you that there are two important qualifications 
on this exception.11 First, it only applies to loans taken on 
the filer's principal place of residence: it does not apply to 
loans taken out on other personal residences. 
Second, it 
applies only to loans from a commercial lending institution 
which are made in the lender's regular course of business on 
terms available to the public without regard to official status: 
it does not apply to personal loans secured by a second deed of 
trust. 
Please feel free to contact me at (916) 322-5901 if I can 
be of further assistance. 
DMF:plh 
Enclosures 
Sincerely, 
~~(1)u,-c(ji;J4~ 
Diane Ma r 
Fishburn~ 
Staff Co 
sel 
Legal Division 
11 
These qualifications presently exist for mortgages 
which are taken out to purchase the filer's principal place of 
residence. 
Section 82030 (b) (8) (A) • 


Designated Employees in category 3 must report: 
Investments, business ositions in, or sources of income 
rom any business entity which has, within the last two 
years, contracted with the department to provide or 
obtain supplies, materials, services, machinery, or 
equipment. 
Designated Employees in Category 4 must report: 
Investments, business positions, or sources of income 
from any business entity which within the last two years 
has been subject to or affected by any regulations or 
policies of the department relating to mobilehomes, manu-
factured homes, factory-built housing, mobilehome parks~ 
labor cam S, recreational vehicles, commercial coaches, 
or any other programs or pol~c~es a 
~nistered by the 
division. 
NOTE: 
Authority cited: 
Section 87300, Government Code. 
Reference: 
Sections 87300-87311, Government Code.

sections.question_synthetic: Senator Becky Morgan sought guidance on her financial disclosure obligations under the Political Reform Act, including reporting requirements for a vacation cabin, stock sales, and various investments and income sources.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: FPPC staff advised that vacation cabins used exclusively for personal family use are not reportable; stock sales should be listed on Schedule A with disposition dates and Schedule D with sale amounts; government-issued bonds and SEC-registered stock dividends are not reportable; and second mortgage refinancing on a principal residence is excluded from income only when from commercial lenders in their regular course of business.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: No formal QUESTION or CONCLUSION sections present; document is structured as a confirmation letter of a prior telephone conversation. Content is clearly FPPC advice. Document ID A-85-048 appears in letter. Multiple footnotes provide statutory references. The document includes some unrelated text at the end regarding designated employees reporting requirements that appears to be from a different document or attachment.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Senator Becky Morgan sought guidance on her financial disclosure obligations under the Political Reform Act, including reporting requirements for a vacation cabin, stock sales, and various investments and income sources.

CONCLUSION: FPPC staff advised that vacation cabins used exclusively for personal family use are not reportable; stock sales should be listed on Schedule A with disposition dates and Schedule D with sale amounts; government-issued bonds and SEC-registered stock dividends are not reportable; and second mortgage refinancing on a principal residence is excluded from income only when from commercial lenders in their regular course of business.

Section 87206 (as amended by Stats. 1984, Ch. 931). This exception would also apply to any other personal residence including a home in Sacramento. embedding.summary: This is an FPPC advice letter confirming guidance provided to Senator Becky Morgan in a February 21, 1985 telephone conversation regarding her financial disclosure obligations, including reporting requirements for personal residences, stock transactions, bond investments, and mortgage refinancing.
